summary.intent: Add per-developer AI usage attribution to the metrics dashboard, plus date range filtering and a chart alignment fix.
summary.outcome: Developer identity is fetched from the GitHub Commits API and surfaced in a dropdown and 'AI Usage by Developer' chart; date range pickers filter all charts; header layout was restructured into rows to fix alignment; FTR sparkline was changed to cumulative rate.

repo: checkpoint_ids are git commit SHA prefixes — author info can be resolved via GitHub Commits API during the fetch step
MUI date picker package (@mui/x-date-pickers) is not installed; native HTML date inputs via MUI TextField type='date' are used instead
The project is a single-developer repo (all 19 checkpoints map to 'Dmytro Shamsiiev'), but the author feature is designed for multi-dev teams

code.finding: Author field is fetched from the GitHub Commits API after the tree API call and mapped by commit SHA prefix; error checkpoints fall back to 'unknown'
Normalizer backfills author field from checkpoint data
Header restructured into 3 rows: (1) metadata + refresh button, (2) View/Developer/From/To filter dropdowns, (3) status message — fixes wrapping alignment issue
FTR sparkline changed from rolling average of binary 0/100 values to cumulative rate so the last point matches the headline stat exactly
author field added to checkpoint data model

workflow: Playwright MCP was used to screenshot the running dashboard and verify layout fixes visually before committing
Build + test run used as a gate after each multi-file change batch

friction: Developer dropdown initially caused the filter row to wrap to a second line, requiring a header layout restructure
FTR sparkline showed 66.7% while headline stat showed 33.3% — rolling average vs. cumulative rate mismatch was a subtle bug that required user to flag it
open_items: fetch-checkpoints.ts script needs to be re-run with a GitHub token to backfill author fields in the cache for existing checkpoints
Single-developer project means the Developer filter and 'AI Usage by Developer' chart are not yet exercised with real multi-author data
